seo.title: Software Developer — Websites & Apps across Morocco | Omar Bougma
seo.description: I build websites, internal tools, and automation for small businesses across Morocco — based in Marrakesh. Fixed quotes, plain language, and software you fully own.

hero.heading: I build websites and tools that get small businesses more customers and
hero.sub: Experience building and running real software since 2022 — now helping small businesses across Morocco get results without the jargon. Based in Marrakesh.

about.heading: The real reason I do this.
about.body: I want a shop owner to spend their evenings with family instead of fighting spreadsheets. Most small businesses already do good work — they just lose hours to clunky tools and lose customers who can't find them. I build the websites and tools that take that friction away, so you get more of the right customers and more of your time back. That's the whole reason I do this.

work.title: Building and running real systems.
work.intro: Since 2022 I've worked across three disciplines at once — writing the software, running the infrastructure it ships on, and keeping it secure. I own problems from the first idea to production, and have learned how systems actually survive contact with real users.

highlights.label: Built the product
highlights.desc: Full-stack development end to end — frontend, backend, and the data in between, designed as one connected whole.
highlights.metric: Metric pending — e.g. shipped [N] features across [N] production apps.

highlights.label: Ran the infrastructure
highlights.desc: DevOps from deployment to monitoring — shipping features into production reliably and keeping them running as people relied on them every day.
highlights.metric: Metric pending — e.g. [N]% uptime · [N] deploys per week.

highlights.label: Kept it secure
highlights.desc: Treated security as part of the build, not an afterthought — hardening what we shipped against real-world risk.
highlights.metric: Metric pending — e.g. [N] vulnerabilities resolved · zero breaches.

work.note: Now I'm taking what I learned across development, operations, and security and offering it directly to the small businesses that need it most.

services.sub: I don't lead with a technology stack. I start with what you're trying to accomplish and choose whatever makes the most sense — often simpler than you expect.

items.title: Websites & Web Apps
items.desc: From landing pages to full platforms — built to convert, to last, and to be yours after I leave.

items.title: Internal Systems
items.desc: Dashboards, tools, and workflows your team will actually use — designed around how you already think.

items.title: Automation
items.desc: Remove the repetitive. Free your people for the work that actually needs a human behind it.

items.title: Hosting, Maintenance & Security
items.desc: I keep it running — hosting, updates, backups, and security, every month. No vanishing after launch.

services.tiersNote: Plan names and prices are placeholders — final pricing to be set.

tiers.name: Standard
tiers.desc: Everything in Basic, plus monitoring & priority fixes

tiers.name: Premium
tiers.desc: Everything in Standard, plus security hardening & a monthly report

principles: It's not about me. I lead with your world, not my skill list.
You own everything — no lock-in, no jargon, plain language as we go.
I'm measured on your outcome, not on my cleverness.
Honest work, for honest businesses, only.
I build things you can still run after I'm gone.
process.audit: You may have hired a developer who vanished halfway, overcharged, or left you with something only they could touch. That's exactly why I work this way — stated out loud, so it can't surprise either of us.

contact.sub: I start every conversation the same way — understanding your world before I say anything about mine.
contact.orWhatsapp: Or message me on WhatsApp at
contact.discoveryLabel: On our first call, I'll ask:
questions: What made you reach out now?
What's the hardest part of running this, day to day?
What would have to change for this to feel worth it to you?
contact.tip: I'll keep summarizing your world back until you say "that's right." Let "no" stay safe — that's the whole point.
contact.closingNote: "Lead with the why. The what will take care of itself."

projects.title: Things I've built.
projects.intro: A few projects — the problem, what I built, and where each one stands today.

items.name: BlueSky — accounting & budgeting SaaS
items.tag: Financial SaaS · built at a previous employer
items.problem: Companies need one place to handle financial operations, budgeting, and strategic planning — with a forward view of their financial health.
items.built: BlueSky — an all-in-one financial accounting and budget-planning SaaS, covering revenues, expenses, assets, liabilities, and more, from day-to-day tracking to high-level projections.
items.tech: A full-stack SaaS with AI-driven analytics that turn historical data into predictions of financial health.
items.outcome: Built and used in production at a previous employer.

items.name: MeinLedger — personal finance tracker
items.problem: People want a clear, simple way to track personal finances without a bloated app.
items.built: Designed, built, and ran a personal finance tracker — net worth, cash flow, spending by category, accounts, goals, and subscriptions.
items.tech: A full-stack web app, from database to interface, deployed and maintained end to end — entirely solo.
items.outcome: A completed product I designed, shipped, and operated myself.

items.name: Fintbib — medical practice platform
items.problem: Medical practices needed one place to run the patient side of the clinic — not just records, but visits, appointments, and documents too.
items.built: A practice-management platform — patient profiles and history, visits and diagnoses, appointments, document management, and insurance, all in one place.
items.tech: A full-stack web app built privacy- and compliance-first, for medical establishments.
items.outcome: In development, pending regulatory approval before handling real patient data.
items.note: Shown with fictional sample data only.

items.name: Abdellah Bougma — artisan portfolio
items.problem: A Moroccan artisan needed an elegant online home for his craft and gallery.
items.built: A portfolio site — his story, his process, and a gallery of his work — designed around his taste.
items.tech: A custom site, designed and built to match his style.

items.q: What do I need to prepare?
items.a: Very little — your content and a rough sense of what you want. I'll guide the rest in plain language.
Yes. Every site, tool, and account is yours — code and content included. No lock-in, ever.
I can keep it running — hosting, updates, backups, and security — through a monthly care plan. See Services above.
